stem: An engineer takes 15 measurements of body weight using a bathroom scale. The measurements have a mean of 138.0 kg and a standard deviation of 15.1 kg. However, calibration tests reveal that a bathroom scale systematically overestimates the true value by between 2.8% and 6.9%. Estimate the true body weight and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 138.0 kg. After correcting for the 4.8% bias: ≈ 131.7 kg. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.8%-6.9%) and sampling variability: [121.3, 142.0]. Point estimate: 131.7 kg.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
